task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: 04-T-001 Loose nozzle plate detected (inform the key operator/customer)

abstract.p: You received this loose nozzle plate (LNP) trigger on your worklist as information to prepare your visit, shorten your diagnostic time and to inform the key operator to prevent future loose nozzle plate issues.

section.title: Preparation
section.p: During job preparation, this loose nozzle plate knowledge should be used to arrange service parts up-front. E.g. Prevent a second visit on a missing printhead.

note.p: Be aware and inform the customer that PH replacements caused by a loose nozzle plate are not covered by warranty nor by service contract (depending on the service contract for example Procare+ covers the replacements of 1 printhead/year).

section.title: Main causes of loose nozzle plate issues

li.p: - Update of maintenance video 5 "Clean the matrix plate"
Show and instruct the key operator.
- Important facts with respect to printhead maintenance and printhead damage.
Show and instruct the key operator.

section.p: The trigger is based on off-line processed ORS printhead information, which was captured in the functional logging during printing over the mentioned period in the trigger.
Each time the offline algorithm detects a loose nozzle plate for a specific printhead, such trigger is generated and will be added to your work list.

The first trigger appearance in your ADAM work list does not per definition indicate that the loose nozzle plate occurrence happened in the last 2 week period. Loose nozzle plate initiated in the past are now found since the loose nozzle plate trigger mechanism is in place.
The trigger mechanism is being rolled-out on a pilot basis and some printers may have a loose nozzle plate over a longer period without knowing.
The trigger will be automatically be removed when no loose nozzle plate is detected anymore.
